title: Piquant Vietnamese Rice Com Tam
description: A spicy and kid-friendly Vietnamese dish featuring vegetable oil and nigiri-shaping. This macrobiotic-friendly recipe is perfect for a quick meal.
instructions: Rinse the jasmine rice in a fine mesh strainer until the water runs clear.
In a large saucepan, combine the water, pork bones, onion, garlic, and ginger.
Bring the mixture to a boil over high heat, then reduce the heat to low and simmer for 10 minutes.
Using a pair of chopsticks, shape the cooked jasmine rice into small balls, about 1 inch in diameter.
Heat 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat.
Add the shaped rice balls to the skillet and cook until they are golden brown on all sides.
Add the remaining 1 tablespoon of vegetable oil, fish sauce, sugar, and chili peppers to the skillet.
Stir-fry for 2 minutes, then add the cooked rice balls back to the skillet.
Toss everything together until the rice balls are well coated with the sauce.
Transfer the com tam to a serving platter and garnish with chopped peanuts.
